message:
feat(widgets): enhance command palette fuzzy scoring algorithm

Major improvements to the command palette fuzzy matching algorithm for
better relevance ranking and more intuitive search results.

scorer.rs changes:
- Implement Smith-Waterman inspired local alignment scoring
- Add consecutive character bonus for contiguous matches
- Introduce word boundary detection with bonus scoring
- Support camelCase and snake_case identifier splitting
- Add acronym matching (e.g., "gf" matches "GitFlow")
- Penalize gaps between matched characters proportionally
- Improve Unicode handling for international identifiers

mod.rs integration:
- Update CommandPalette to use enhanced scoring pipeline
- Add score normalization for consistent ranking across query lengths
- Implement tie-breaking by command name length (shorter = better)
- Support configurable scoring weights via PaletteConfig
- Add highlight span calculation for matched character visualization
- Cache scoring results for repeated queries on same command set

Scoring formula:
  base_score = sum(char_match_score)
  + consecutive_bonus * consecutive_matches
  + boundary_bonus * boundary_matches
  + acronym_bonus * acronym_matches
  - gap_penalty * total_gap_length

Performance characteristics:
- O(n*m) where n=query length, m=candidate length
- Early exit for impossible matches (query longer than candidate)
- Incremental scoring for prefix queries

Example improvements:
- "open" now ranks "Open File" above "Developer Options"
- "gco" matches "git checkout" via acronym detection
- "usr" prefers "user" over "cursor" (word boundary)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
